A brief EF1 tornado with peak wind speeds of 100 mph produced tree and pockets of structural damage starting 5 miles north of Broken Bow, just on the west side of Highway 259. The 200 yard wide tornado was only on the ground for approximately half a mile and fortunately produced no injuries or fatalities.

A weak EF-0 tornado with peak winds of 85 mph started on the east shore of the Toledo Bend reservoir and tracked a little under 4 miles to the northeast, ending in the vicinity of the intersection of Highway 191 and Prospect Road. Nearly all damage was to tress and there were no casualties. Max width of 440 yards.

An EF1 tornado touched down on the southwest side of Shreveport along Cedar Creek Drive and tracked just over 3 miles through residential areas where it snapped and uprooted numerous trees. The roof of a small outbuilding at Southwood High School was also completely removed at which point maximum winds were estimated at 95 mph and tornado width at 350 yards as it passed overhead. The tornado continued northeast across Walker Road, Mackey Lane and Jewella Avenue before finally lifting after crossing LA Highway 3132 just east of Mansfield Road.
Trees and power lines down on Sara Lane.
Substantial damage to an apartment building near the 4300 block N Market St.
An NWS storm survey confirmed straight line winds of approximately 70 mph removed most of the roof covering to an apartment complex building on North Market Street.
Trees snapped on Tynneside Way in South Shreveport.
Reported at KDTN...Downtown Shreveport Airport ASOS.

An EF0 tornado touched down along Malone Road and tracked north northeast for nearly 3 miles across LA Highway 537 and then Still House Road before lifting. Along its path, the tornado uprooted hardwood and softwood trees and broke several large branches with maximum winds estimated at 85 mph and a width of 300 yards.
A power pole was snapped with a power line pulled out of a home at MM 6 on Highway 71 South.
Trees down on Highway 160 just past CR 195 east of Doddridge.

An EF1 tornado began near the intersection of Still House Road and Log Ferry Road and tracked northeast, paralleling Wise Road for a few miles before crossing Clements Road. The tornado produced mainly EF-0 tree damage in the form of uproots and downed limbs. However, there were also quite a few tree trunk snaps as it crossed Clements Road with a peak wind speed estimated at 100 mph and a width of 325 yards.

An EF0 tornado began along Highway 157 and tracked north northeast, crossing Chalybeate Springs Road and ending shortly thereafter. The tornado produced mainly tree damage in the form of uproots and downed limbs with estimated maximum winds of 85 mph and a width of 275 yards.

An EF1 tornado tracked east, along and just south of County Road 7 in the far southwest portion of Lafayette County. Despite the short path, the tornado produced higher end EF-1 damage with maximum estimated wind speeds of 110 mph attributed to snapping of power poles and nearby destruction of a metal barn housing hay bales. Otherwise, damage was mostly to trees in the form of uproots, trunk snaps, and downing of large limbs.
*** 1 INJ *** A male on Lafayette Road 245 on the south side of Lake Erling was hospitalized with non life threatening injuries due to a large tree falling into a residence. Time estimated by radar.
Highway 71 is blocked due to several trees down.
Power poles and lines down on CR 246 on the south end of Lake Erling.

An EF1 tornado tracked northeast to the west of Lake Erling, starting along Hood Road and ending just after crossing Highway 160. The tornado could have begun slightly before Hood Road, but many trees crossing that road limited the survey teams access farther to the southwest. Damage was mostly to trees in the form of uproots, trunk snaps, and downing of large limbs with maximum estimated wind speeds of 90 mph and a width of 150 yards.

A weak tornado began near Rocky Spring Rd northeast of Many. The tornado continued east, eventually crossing Rocky Mount Rd and Cedar grove Rd before ending near the Sabine/Natchitoches Parish line. Along the path, trees were uprooted and tree trunks were snapped, indicating EF-1 level damage. The path width was around 325 yards with a path length of around 5 miles.

This is a continuation of the Southeast Henderson County tornado. This tornado moved across Lake Palestine as a waterspout as it crossed into extreme Southwest Smith County into Dogwood City along Highway 155, where a few small trees were uprooted and multiple large limbs were snapped. This tornado and associated damage continued to narrow as it crossed Highway 155, but still topped a few trees along CR 1170, snapping several large branches and partially snapping a power pole, downing a power line across Lakeway Circle. The tornado then lifted as it moved into an inlet along Lake Palestine just west of Emerald Bay. The estimated maximum winds associated with the entire tornado track from Southeast Henderson County was 105 mph, although winds in extreme Southwest Smith County were estimated near 85 mph. The estimated maximum path width of the tornado was 480 yards, which was found in the Briarwood Bay subdivision in Coffee City in extreme Southeast Henderson County.
Bullard Fire Department reporting trees and power lines down.
Public social media report of trees and large limbs down in Emerald Bay area.

A NWS Survey determined straight-line wind damage in the Pleasant Hill and Union Grove communities just south of the town of Haworth. Damage consisted of mostly of downed trees and power lines, with structural damage to a chicken farm house, and a few outbuildings and sheds.
One foot of water over highway 70 in Valliant.

An EF-1 tornado, with peak winds estimated at 110 mph, first touched down on Beacon Hill Road in rural Central Sevier County, AR between the towns of De Queen and Lockesburg. It continued to move northeastward for several miles, crossing US Highway 71 just south of the US Hwy 70 intersection, then lifting along Sevier County Road 328. Damage consisted mostly of downed trees and power lines, with structural damage to a few outbuidlings and sheds. Max width was estimated to be 300 yards, with a path length of around 8.3 miles. No deaths or injuries were reported.

A supercell thunderstorm moved into Bowie County in far Northeast Texas around 7 PM CDT on Friday May 24, 2024. This supercell thunderstorm began to rotate shortly thereafter north of the city of New Boston. Evidence of tree trunks snapped associated with weak tornadic touchdown (EF1, winds around 93 mph) were located along Daniels Chapel Road between County Roads 3108 and 3011 around 7:05 PM CDT. The weak tornado then continued southeast crossing Daniels Chapel Road again near the intersections of County Road 3107 and FM 3378 where more snapped tree trunks were found. The weak tornado then traveled very closely along Daniels Chapel Road as it continued southeast with several more tree trunks snapped, several trees uprooted, and numerous large tree branches downed. The tornado then lifted around the intersection of Daniels Chapel Road and County Road 3010 around 7:09 PM CDT, with the storm continuing as a severe thunderstorm as it began to move east towards Highway 8 and eventually towards the town of RedBank, Texas. This storm continued to produce strong winds, with evidence of sporadic wind damage throughout this general area.

This EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 100 mph, was part of a line of convection that moved through Nacogdoches County and was embedded in a much wider swath of wind damage. The tornado first touched down southwest of the corner at County Road 302 and County Road 313. The damage in this area consisted of many trees snapped or uprooted that were convergent and more chaotic than the surrounding damage, however, there was some damage to the roofing of a small barn on the property along with a snapped power pole nearby. The tornado then crossed County Road 313 just south of County Road 302 where we saw significant tree damage. From here, the road network was sparse and we were unable to find any other points until the tornado crossed County Road 302 north of San Augustine Highway to the west of Melrose. EF-0 damage continued along County Road 302 south to San Augustine Highway with damage being seen along the highway into Melrose. The tornado then crossed County Road 331 where damage seemed to increase and was consistent with that of EF-1 wind speeds. From here the tornado continued north of San Augustine Highway till it reached FM 3276 before it lifted just to the east of 3276.
Numerous trees and powerlines down across the county.
*** 1 INJ *** A large tree was uprooted with strong winds of up to 90 mph. The tree unfortunately fell on top of a home. During the tree falling on the home, the homeowner sustained a head injury.

Corrected location previous tstm wnd dmg report from 10 S Zwolle. Law enforcement advised reports of numerous trees and power lines downed up and down Hwy 191 and Hwy 171.

This brief and weak EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 90 mph, in association with a quasi-linear convection system (surging thunderstorm squall line), began along Willie Glaze Road and traveled northeast across Friendship Road and Sammy Railey Road before ending along Mounce Road. Damage was almost exclusively to trees and tree damage aligned in different directions confirmed rotation associated with a tornado. There was also a brief radar tornado debris signature in close vicinity to this tornado. Fortunately, there were no injuries.

This brief EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 100 mph, in association with a quasi-linear convection system (surging thunderstorm squall line), began along Mounce Road, a few miles east of where the Friendship Road tornado ended along Mounce Road. It then traveled northeast and heavily affected a small community near Thomas line where one mobile home was destroyed by a large uprooted tree. The tornado then moved across Blunt Mill Road before ending shortly thereafter. Damage was mostly to trees and tree damage aligned in different directions confirmed rotation associated with a tornado. Fortunately, there were no injuries and the destroyed mobile home appeared to be unoccupied.

This brief EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 105 mph, in association with a quasi-linear convection system (surging thunderstorm squall line), began near the intersection of Mounce Road and Blunt Mill Road, less than a mile east of where the previous tornado began. This tornado also traveled northeast and ended in a wooded area east of Blunt Mill Road. Damage was mostly to trees and powerlines and convergent tree damage aligned in different directions confirmed rotation associated with a tornado. Fortunately, there were no injuries.
Trees down across LA Hwy 175 about a mile south of I-49. Time estimated from radar.

This EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph began just east of Highway 175, about 1 mile south of I-49. The tornado advanced roughly 3.9 miles to the north-northeast before lifting, producing a combination of tree and structural damage. Fortunately, there were no injuries or fatalities. Max width of 400 yards.

This brief and weak EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 88 mph began along Gravel Point Road and traveled through a small portion of DeSoto Parish under new development. Damage was almost exclusively to trees and tree damage aligned in different directions confirmed rotation associated with a tornado. Fortunately, there were no injuries or fatalities. Max width of 250 yards.

This brief and weak EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 88 mph began along Bradleigh Blvd and traveled through a small portion of DeSoto Parish under new development. Damage was almost exclusively to trees and tree damage aligned in different directions confirmed rotation associated with a tornado. Fortunately, there were no injuries or fatalities. Max width was 100 yards.
Delayed report of trees and power lines downed across Highway 191 west of Hornbeck.
Water reported over multiple roadways SW of Carthage including CR 106, 108, and 110.
Delayed report of multiple trees downed along Highway 117 in western Natchitoches Parish and Highway 120 between Robeline and Provencal.

An NWS storm survey found thunderstorm winds damage estimated up to 70 mph near the community of Mora. One mobile home received minor roof and fascia damage and there was other tree damage on the property.
Trees down in the southbound lane of US Hwy 71 near Colfax.

Large oak tree uprooted. 100ft of fences blown over near Kingfisher Trail in Stonewall off of Stonewall Frierson Road. Estimated winds of 60 to 70 mph.

Tree reported down between Ashdown and Forman. Time estimated by radar.

Tree down about 5 miles SW of Tatum on Highway 43.
Reports of multiple trees down in Henderson.
Marion County Dispatch confirmed a few buildings on the southeast side of Jefferson had their roofs removed due to high winds. Winds estimated at 70 to 80 mph with time estimated by radar.
Law enforcement received reports of trees down along US Hwy 371 north of Magnolia. Numerous trees reported downed across Columbia county.
SHV Regional Airport reported a gust of 63 mph.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 NNW Magnolia. Law enforcement received reports of trees down along US Hwy 371 north of Magnolia. Numerous trees reported downed across Columbia county.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 NNW Magnolia. Law enforcement received reports of trees down along Hwy 98 near Emerson. Numerous trees reported downed across Columbia county.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Henderson. Reports of multiple trees down in Henderson.
Reports of numerous trees down across the parish: some locations include Harris Road, near LA 2 and Lisbon Landing, and on HWY 79.
Tree reported down along Hwy 5 in DeSoto Parish, LA.
Reports of multiple trees down in Central Heights and Garrison.
Tree down on a powerline at the junction of Highway 371 and Joe Bill Adcock Road. Time estimated by radar.
Large tree down at SFA Gardens.
Tree down near the junction of FM 343 and Highway 69. Time estimated by radar.
Reports of multiple trees down across the town of Campti.
Report of tree down on Holliman Road near HWY 146.
Report of multiple trees down in the town of Chireno.
Reports of multiple trees down across the town of Robeline.
Two trees down near the intersection of 87 North and Telephone Road. Time estimated by radar.
Several reports of trees down in and around Lufkin. Time estimated by radar.
Two trees were downed onto power lines near the intersection of FM 58 and Southern Drive.
Trees down on Bethlehem Road in Hudson.
Trees down in the 4600 block of Highway 69 north just northwest of Lufkin.
Trees down in the 900 block of Old Highway 59.

TS Beryl spawned this EF2 tornado which began along Hwy 174 west of Converse. Most of the damage consisted of snapped and uprooted hardwood and softwood trees as the tornado moved northwest from Sabine Parish into De Soto Parish. The most significant damage occurred to a church near the intersection of Hwy 539 and Hwy 481 southwest of Mansfield. The original wooden church building dating back to the 1900s lost much of its roof and the exterior walls of the southern half of the building collapsed. Just to the west, a Metal Building System housing a gymnasium was almost completely destroyed with the steel I-beams were pulled free from their anchors. Most of the anchoring bolts were still in the foundation. This damage was rated EF2. The tornado crossed NNW, crossing Hwy 84 near Hunter Road and lifted near the intersection of Cedar Hill Road and Blunt Mill Road. Path length was approx. 17 miles with estimated max. wind speed of 125 mph.

An EF-1 tornado with estimated maximum winds near 105 mph touched down about 1.5 miles west of Alto along Highway 294, where numerous trees were snapped and uprooted. A single family home along Highway 294 had its metal roof peeled off, with numerous adjacent trees snapped and uprooted. The tornado then lifted shortly thereafter in an open pasture near CR 2328. The estimated path length of the tornado was 1.13 miles, with a path width of 200 yards.

An NWS Survey Team determined that a tornado touched down in Sabine County Texas, first touching down over Toledo Bend south of Holly Park Marina. The tornado caused significant damage to a number of RVs and trees at the Holly Park Marina. The tornado then went back out over the water and came back onshore near Nine Mile Road. It then followed the shoreline through the land of a private residence, bringing down additional trees. It is hard to tell if the tornado lifted at this point, but we do believe it went back over the water and lifted before making contact with land anywhere else.

An NWS Storm Survey determined that an EF-1 tornado touched down about 2 miles ESE of the community of Appleby where it first started to produce damage on County Road 260 before tracking to the northwest for about 8 miles before lifting just east of Highway 259 north of the Mahl Community.

An NWS Survey Team determined that an EF-0 tornado with peak winds of 85 mph touched down along S Highway 259 about 8 miles to the SSE of Henderson in Rusk County TX. The tornado caused damage to a number of trees during the 3.75 mile path before lifting about 5.5 miles to the south of Henderson near County Road 377 and County Road 314.

This EF-1 tornado tracked primarily through the northern portion of the Sabine National Forest and produced primarily timber damage. The peak estimated wind speed was 105 mph, peak width of 375 yards, and a path length of 6.7 miles.

Corrects start time of previous tornado report from 1 W Converse. TS Beryl spawned this EF2 tornado which began along Hwy 174 west of Converse. Most of the damage consisted of snapped and uprooted hardwood and softwood trees as the tornado moved northwest from Sabine Parish into De Soto Parish. The most significant damage occurred to a church near the intersection of Hwy 539 and Hwy 481 southwest of Mansfield. The original wooden church building dating back to the 1900s lost much of its roof and the exterior walls of the southern half of the building collapsed. Just to the west, a Metal Building System housing a gymnasium was almost completely destroyed with the steel I-beams were pulled free from their anchors. Most of the anchoring bolts were still in the foundation. This damage was rated EF2. The tornado crossed NNW, crossing Hwy 84 near Hunter Road and lifted near the intersection of Cedar Hill Road and Blunt Mill Road. Path length was approx. 17 miles with estimated max. wind speed of 125 mph.
Possible tornado damage. Several buildings damaged in Timpson, including City Hall. Roof removed from City Hall. Several trees down, and 2 cars thrown at the intersection of Hwy 59 and 84.

An NWS Survey determined that a strong EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph first touched down in Timpson Texas just south of N 1st street between Jacob Street and Todd street. The tornado traveled to the northwest for around 2.5 miles before lifting along FM 1970 N just to the north of US Highway 84 W.

NWS Storm Survey team determined that an EF-2 tornado touched down in northeast Sabine Parish and continued on a north northwest track into the eastern half of DeSoto Parish. The tornado was particularly damaging in the Pleasant Hill community in far northeast Sabine Parish where numerous trees were snapped. Further north into DeSoto Parish, the tornado destroyed two single-wide manufactured homes. Two houses sustained significant damage and another single-wide manufactured home was destroyed near the intersection of Hwy. 346 and Cross Road. The storm finally lifted near the Carmel community.

An NWS Storm Survey concluded that an EF-1 tornado first touched down near the community of Pelican, Louisiana, moving northwest and nearly paralleling Hwy 513 before lifting just south southeast of the community of Oxford, Louisiana. Damage consisted of several uprooted and/or snapped trees near the Pelican community and large limbs snapped further north near Oxford.

NWS Storm Survey Team determined that a continuous EF1 tornado track began in far northwest DeSoto Parish, moved northwest into Caddo Parish as well as extreme northeast Panola County and southeast Harrison County. Damage was extensive along this 26 mile track with numerous trees snapped and/or uprooted, powerlines down and power poles snapped. Several structures were damaged by fallen trees with perhaps the worst damage witnessed in and around the Bethany community in far southwest Caddo Parish.

An NWS Storm Survey Team found a brief EF-0 tornado with peak winds of 85 mph touched down near the East Texas Regional Airport southeast of Longview TX. This tornado first touched down along Lake Road, just to the west of FM 2011 E on Lake Cherokee where some minor tree damage was observed. The tornado traveled to the northwest for about 3 miles, producing minor tree damage along the way. As it crossed over the airport, it cause some damage to a number of hangars on the southwest side of the airport. The tornado lifted shortly after crossing State Highway 322 N.

A NWS Storm Survey Team found a strong EF-1 tornado touched down about 4 miles to the northeast of Tatum, TX in Panola County. The tornado first touched down along FM 959 just south of State Highway 43 where it caused significant tree damage and some house damage. It also took a small, well built shed and rolled it a few times. The tornado then tracked to the northwest for about 9.75 miles before lifting shortly after crossing W FM 968 about 1.6 miles south of I-20.

Tropical Storm Beryl spawned this EF1 tornado with peak winds of 95 mph, which started along the Red River just north of the confluence with the Sulphur River in SE Miller County. A metal barn near the start point did suffer the loss of a few metal roof panels. Otherwise, the remainder of the damage consisted of uprooted and snapped hardwood and softwood trees with numerous large limbs downed. It generally travelled in a meandering north and northwest direction, crossing Interstate 49 and US Hwy 71, just NE of the Sulphur River Wildlife Management Area. Path length was 6.39 miles with a peak width of 619 yards.

TS Beryl spawned this EF1 tornado with est. peak winds of 100 mph. It began southeast of Ninock near the Bossier/Red River Parish line. Most of the damage consisted of snapped and uprooted trees and downed limbs, some of which fell on structures. The tornado entered the SE of corner of Barksdale AFB just north of Sligo. The tornado track ends here pending a more thorough survey of Barksdale AFB.
Reports of likely tornado damage off of Horton Road. Radar had TDS at the time.
Video of a tornado near Elm Grove sent via social media. Time and position estimated from TDS via radar.

An NWS Survey Team found that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 107 mph touched down along Farm-to-Market Road 134 south of Leigh, TX. It travelled just east of Bill Coleman Road snapping and uprooting hardwood and softwood trees before turning north, crossing Marshall Leigh Road, and travelling north again. At its peak, the tornado had a max width of 270 yards, ultimately dissipating near the intersection of Baldwin Road and Peter Chapel Road in Harrison County after traveling 10.03 miles.

A storm chaser captured video of a tornado that was on the ground over an open field west of Highway 1 about 3 miles south-southeast of the Powhatan community. However, an NWS Survey Team was unable to find damage indicators to rate this tornado before it lifted prior to reaching Highway 485 and thus, the tornado was rated as an EF-Unknown. The estimated path length was 2.25 miles.
Video of a tornado sent via social media.

*** 1 FATAL, 2 INJ *** TS Beryl spawned this EF1 tornado with peak wind speeds of 100 mph. This may be a continuation of the tornado that first touched down south of Barksdale AFB pending an additional survey of the base itself. Otherwise, the tornado began near I-20 and Hwy 79 west of Haughton. Numerous trees were snapped and/or uprooted, damaging some structures. Numerous power poles were also snapped. One fatality and two injuries occurred on East Linton Road east of Cypress Bayou Reservoir when a tree fell on a mobile home. The tornado travelled northwest and lifted just north of Hwy 2 west of Plain Dealing. Corrects previous tornado report from 6 E Benton. Bossier Parish Sheriff reports a tree fell onto a mobile home just east of Benton near Cypress Black Bayou. Path length was 26.3 miles with a max. width of 1200 yards.

A very brief EF-0 tornado with estimated maximum winds near 85 mph touched down along Jessie Latin Road, where several small trees and large limbs were snapped. The tornado lifted quickly thereafter. The tornado path length was 0.40 miles, with a path width of 200 yards.
Substantial damage to pool house and two big pine trees downed along Meadowchase Trail.
*** 1 FATAL *** Bossier Parish Sheriff reports a tree fell onto a mobile home just east of Benton near Cypress Black Bayou.

An NWS Survey Team found that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph touched down about 1.5 miles to the southeast of Shelbyville in Shelby County, Texas. The tornado did damage to both hardwood and softwood trees, as well as to a number of homes along its 10.39-mile path. At its peak, the tornado had a max width of 900 yards, ultimately dissipating near the Jericho Creek in Shelby County.

This corrects a previous LSR starting at 3:29 PM CDT in Sabine County TX to reflect a slightly earlier and farther south starting point based on aerial and satellite imagery. This strong EF-1 did primarily tree damage and limited structural damage with peak winds of 110 mph and a maximum width of 650 yards. This tornado was on the ground for 5 miles.

An NWS Storm Survey Team determined another tornado touched down near the community of East Hamilton on the west side of Toledo Bend. Initial thoughts are that the tornado first started over the water, coming onshore near a boat loading location at the end of FM 2261 where it twisted and uprooted a large amount of trees. Drone footage obtained confirmed that the path tracked for 4 miles to the north northwest, causing significant tree damage before lifting shortly after crossing FS Road 141 southwest of County Road 2941.

An NWS Storm Survey Team determined that a short EF-1 tornado started along Piney Woods Road about 4 miles southwest of Bienville, LA in Bienville Parish. The tornado was on the ground for about 3.25 miles and produced mainly tree damage throughout the path before lifting along Highway 507.

An NWS Survey Team determined that a EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 100 mph first touched down about 1.5 miles miles east of Paxton TX just to the north of US Highway 84 E near County Road 3831. The tornado caused a trail of damage, mainly to trees, along its 5 mile path before lifting near County Road 4371 and County Road 437.

This short EF-1 tornado track was confirmed by available high resolution satellite data and a radar tornadic debris signature that originated at the point of the satellite damage signature. There was no available ground access to any NWS survey team. Peak wind damage assumed to be at least 100 mph given the substantial tree damage signature on the mentioned high resolution satellite data. Path width was 400 yards with a path length of 1.28 miles.

TS Beryl spawned this EF1 tornado with peak winds of 95 mph. It began SW of Plain Dealing near Dutch John Road and travelled NW, crossing Hwy 2 and Hwy 537, where it snapped numerous trees before lifting just north of Hwy 537.

*** 1 INJ *** An EF-2 tornado with max winds of 115 mph touched down on the east side of Toledo Bend in far NW Sabine Parish along Camp Road. The most significant damage and the one injury was reported on Woodlake Drive where a home was heavily damaged.

Tropical Storm Beryl spawned this brief EF0 tornado, which began on Arkansas Line Road just south of Horneman Road. The tornado moved northwest uprooting some trees and downing large limbs before lifting along McCall Road. Peak winds with this tornado were 80 MPH, with peak width being 50 yards.

NWS Survey Team found damage related to an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 105 mph. The tornado caused the most damage along Grider Road just south of Highway 79 northeast of Minden. A large tree fell on a home there and caused significant damage. Thankfully no injuries were reported.

An EF-1 tornado with estimated maximum winds of 105 mph touched down near the intersection of Log Ferry Road and Arkansas Line Road in far Northern Bossier Parish, where several trees were snapped. The tornado then crossed into Southwest Lafayette County Arkansas east of County Road 1, remaining over mostly open fields before snapping/bending several wooden power poles along County Road 1 just south of Lafayette 47. The tornado also flattened a portion of a corn field in this same area, before emerging back over open fields and lifting just before reaching Highway 160 west of Gin City. The estimated path length of the tornado was 6.98 miles, with a path width of 300 yards.

An NWS Survey Team found that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph touched down between CR 2993 and CR 2915 east of Hughes Springs. The tornado did damage to both hardwood and softwood trees, as well as a few power poles and older farm buildings. At its peak, the tornado had a max width of 700.00 yards, ultimately dissipating near the town of Hughes Springs.

A strong EF-1 tornado first touched down between Converse and Oak Grove, Louisiana in far northern Sabine Parish. The storm moved almost due north, snapping and uprooting numerous trees along its northward path. The storm continued into southern DeSoto Parish, crossing Hwy. 512 and then north northwest towards Judson Darby Road before lifting. Some structures were damaged by fallen trees and limbs along the storms path as well across northern Sabine and southern DeSoto Parishes as well as downing several power lines.

Corrects previous tornado report from 7 NE Minden. A brief EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 105 mph touched down near the intersection of Highway 70 and Dogwood Trail in Webster Parish about 7 miles to the NE of Minden. Damage was mainly seen to trees, but in one field, the damage was very significant with over 15 trees snapped or uprooted. The tornado path length was 1.53 miles, with a path width of 345 yards.

A brief EF-0 tornado with estimated maximum winds near 74 mph touched down along Lafayette Road 47, downing some large tree limbs. The tornado lifted in a large agricultural field between Lafayette Road 47 and Highway 160 east of County Road 57.

Corrects time of previous tornado report from 8 SSE Fouke. Tropical Storm Beryl spawned this EF1 tornado with peak winds of 95 mph, which started along the Red River just north of the confluence with the Sulphur River in SE Miller County. A metal barn near the start point did suffer the loss of a few metal roof panels. Otherwise, the remainder of the damage consisted of uprooted and snapped hardwood and softwood trees with numerous large limbs downed. It generally travelled in a meandering north and northwest direction, crossing Interstate 49 and US Hwy 71, just NE of the Sulphur River Wildlife Management Area. Path length was 6.39 miles with a peak width of 619 yards.
Law enforcement reports tree down in Hughes Springs. Tornado possible.

A short lived EF-1 tornado with estimated maximum winds near 90 mph touched down just south of the Grand Cane Oil Field, where it snapped/uprooted a number of pine trees along Radio Station Road near the intersection of Parish Road 274. A few large trees were also snapped and uprooted along Blunt Mill Road and Phill Davis Road before lifting. The tornado path length was 2.54 miles, with a path width of 67 yards.

A short lived EF-1 tornado with estimated maximum winds near 90 mph touched down just south of the Grand Cane Oil Field, where it snapped/uprooted a number of pine trees along Radio Station Road near the intersection of Parish Road 274. A few large trees were also snapped and uprooted along Blunt Mill Road and Phill Davis Road before lifting. The tornado path length was 2.54 miles, with a path width of 67 yards.

An NWS Survey Team found that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph touched down about 4 miles to the SE of Shongaloo near the Dykesville Community. Snapped and uprooted trees along with a few power lines were down along the 4.75 mile path before lifting near Dixie Fomby Road road in eastern Webster Parish Louisiana.

NWS Storm Survey Team determined that a brief EF-0 tornado touched down to the west and southwest of Carmel, Louisiana near Hwy 509 where it downed large tree limbs. The storm continued north and then northwest before lifting near Sloan Road, southeast of Holly and Kingston, Louisiana.

Tropical Storm Beryl spawned this brief EF1 tornado with peak winds of 100 mph. It began along County Road 220 and travelled north to just west of County Road 25. It turned to the northwest and crossed County Road 219 and lifted along Miller County 20. The damage consisted entirely of snapped trees and large downed limbs.
Shongaloo firefighter reports tree down on vehicle on Sand Hill Road.

An NWS Storm Survey Team determined that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 105 mph touched down 3 mile north northeast of Shongaloo in Webster Parish Louisiana. The tornado first started producing damage along on Sand Hill Road where multiple trees were snapped or uprooted. One tree caused some damage to a car and a shed was damaged. The tornado was on the ground for around 6.5 miles before it lifted in Columbia County Arkansas near Plainfield.
Shongaloo firefighter reports tornado confirmed on the ground at the Louisiana-Arkansas state line along Hwy 19/159.

NWS Survey Team concluded the damage SW of Magnolia was not tornadic, due to a lack of a convergence pattern. Peak winds in the wind swath were estimated to be around 85 mph with fortunately little to no structural damage observed and no injuries or fatalities reported.

Corrects previous tornado report from 2 ENE Shongaloo. This EF1 tornado started just south of Highway 2 Alternate and initially damaged a metal shed and a couple trees. As the tornado moved generally north, tree damage was noted along the path as it crossed Mike Pope Road, Gravel Pit Road and Tom Hayes Road. The tornado dissipated shortly after crossing Sand Hill Road. Max winds were around 95 mph. Path width was 400 yards. Path length was 3.9 miles.

Tropical Storm Beryl spawned this brief EF1 tornado between King and Celeste Avenues just north of US Hwy 82 in Texarkana, TX. Most of the damage was to hardwood and softwood trees. Significant structural damage occurred in this neighborhood with numerous trees falling on homes and vehicles. Some homes did have small portions of their roofing uplifted as did an older retail building. The tornado traveled in an arcing path from east to west and ended just before Beverly Park. Peak winds were 90 mph.

A brief EF-1 tornado with estimated maximum winds near 95 mph touched down along Wallace Lake near the Desoto/Caddo Parish line, and quickly moved northwest across a large wooded area just south of the Southern Trace subdivision, where multiple hardwood trees were snapped and uprooted. The tornado then moved across the southwest fringes of Southern Trace along and behind Saint Francis Way, where numerous small trees were uprooted, large branches were snapped, a few fences blown down, and some minor shingle damage was noted to several homes. The tornado ripped the metal roof off of the guard station at the entrance of Southern Trace, before lifting shortly thereafter over a wooded area between Wallace Lake Road and I-49 just south of Southern Loop. The estimated tornado path length was 1.86 miles, with a path width of 150 yards.

NWS Storm Survey Team determined that a weak EF-1 tornado started just north of the community of Campti just to the west of Highway 486. The tornado then advanced north, crossing over Billy Stiles Road and the Black Lake. Damage was mainly to trees and thankfully there were no injuries. The tornado lifted just to the north of Five Forks Road after a path of around 5.75 miles.

This tornado touched down just north of Highway 2 along Weldon Church Road. As it moved through a wooded area, substantial damage was done to the timber as hundreds of trees were uprooted and snapped. Peak intensity was noted here, EF2 with max winds around 115 mph. Path width was 750 yards. Total path length was 3.2 miles.

This brief tornado started on Lem Road just north of the intersection of Green Bridge Road with a few damaged trees. The tornado moved north and crossed Lem Road again. Here the tornado peaked in intensity as it heavily damaged one home. The home had the roof removed along with some outer walls down. Two pickup trucks were rolled or thrown into the yard. Additional tree damage was noted here with the heaviest tree damage just to the southeast of the home. Path width was 175 yds. Max winds around 125 mph. Path length was 1.5 miles.

Drone footage from the Camden Fire Dept. confirmed an EF1 tornado from TD Beryl began in Ouachita Co AR and moved northwest into Nevada Co AR. A barn suffered major roof damage just south of Hwy 332. The tornado continued northwest and uprooted some trees before lifting just north of CR 25. Path length in Nevada County was 0.54 miles, with a max. width of 50 yards. Est. max. wind speed was 90 mph.

This EF1 tornado started just south of Highway 2 Alternate and initially damaged a metal shed and a couple trees. As the tornado moved generally north, tree damage was noted along the path as it crossed Mike Pope Road, Gravel Pit Road and Tom Hayes Road. The tornado dissipated shortly after crossing Sand Hill Road. Max winds were around 95 mph. Path width was 400 yards. Path length was 3.9 miles.

Video and photos confirm the development of a Gustnado shortly after a strong gust front/outflow boundary moved across the town of Taylor, Arkansas. This feature moved a little more than a quarter of a mile across the town uprooting several large trees and snapping numerous large branches.

A brief EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 105 mph touched down near the intersection of Highway 70 and Dogwood Trail in Webster Parish about 7 miles to the NE of Minden. Damage was mainly seen to trees, but in one field, the damage was very significant with over 15 trees snapped or uprooted.

Delayed Report...Numerous tree branches were snapped, a glass table was shattered, and a sun awning was ripped off a trailer on a residence just off of FM Road 1971 on County Road 164 in the Dotson/Long Branch area.
Two bridges on Highway 490 north of Janie Gorum Road were inundated with high water.
Water over a portion of South Main Street in Zwolle. Numerous other secondary roads in the area are also cover by high water.
Water over HWY 2026 in Center.
Reports of flooding on Highway 2213 and FM 705 near San Augustine.
Reports of water over roadways on Highway 5 in Logansport and Highway 84 between Logansport and Mansfield.
Report of flooding on FM3150 near Jacks Creek.
Report of flooding along FM2109 near Hanks Creek.
Report of flooding on Highway 9 near Campti.
Reports of flooding on Highway 494 and Highway 3191 near downtown Natchitoches.
Report of flooding on Cochran Rd east/southeast of Huntington, TX.
Tree downed across Highway 84 near the post office in Umpire.
Trees and power lines downed near the intersection of Tollett Road and Bissell Road.
Trees downed along Tollett Road just south of the intersection with Hill Loop.
Updating tstm wnd dmg report from 9 NNE Dierks. Tree downed along Tollett Road just south of the intersection with Hill Loop, falling onto a home, two vehicles and a storage shed.
Tree Down SW of Marion Louisiana.
Two trees down near Farmerville.
Multiple trees reported down in association with thunderstorm outflow boundary moving on the north and northeast side of Henderson.
Trees down near Columbia, Clarks, and Grayson along with 1 inch hail.
Law enforcement reported at least one tree down at the intersection of Old Plain Dealing Road and Abe Martin Road. Time estimated by radar.
Metal roof and sheet-metal wall damage at Southeast Autoplex and nearby casino in downtown Idabel. Car windows blown out.

This EF1 tornado began in an inaccessible area north of the Little River and west of US Hwy 70 per storm chaser video. The tornado traveled north-northeast and an NWS Survey Team confirmed EF1 damage consisting of snapped hardwood trees along Tebo Jones Road. The tornado continued NE crossing Wood Turtle Lane and downed a few large tree limbs along Rodeo Road before lifting. Path length was 3.09 miles. Max. width was 150 yards. Est. peak wind was 93 mph.
Trees down and sheet metal blown off of a building.

An EF1 tornado began NW of Boxelder TX just west of Crystal Lake. Sporadic damage consisting of large tree limbs downed and snapped trunks from both hardwood and softwood trees were observed. The tornado traveled NE crossing FM 44, FM 1701, CR 4310, CR 4315, and US Hwy 82 before lifting along CR 3322. Path length was 7.89 miles with a max. width of 275 yards. Est. peak wind was 90 mph.
Extensive damage to a house on CR3322 NW of Avery.
High water across Highway 127 near the intersection of Pinehill Road just northwest of Jena.
Highway 500 was flooded in the Searcy community.
High water covering Highway 165 and Crawford Loop just south of Pollock.
High water covers Highway 167 and Thigpen Road near the Bentley community.
High water covers Highway 167 near Jades Barber Shop along the Grant/Rapides Parish line.
The intersection of Highway 122 and 123 was flooded near Dry Prong.
Hugart Road near Highway 8 in Pollock was flooded.
Mayo Road south of Highway 8 was covered in high water.
Hooper Road near Gone Wild Safari was flooded.
Flagon Road near Neal Road was flooded.
Water over Provo Road.
Water over several roads across the city of Prescott.
Public reported.
Reports of 2-3 power lines sparking in the area.

This relatively brief EF-1 tornado likely began in the midst of the Toledo Bend Reservoir and produced lower end structural damage to residences and boat houses along the shore. There was also a plethora of tree damage consistent with an EF-1 tornado. The tornado ended in the vicinity of LA Highway 1215. Peak width was 220 yards and peak wind speed was 100 mph. Path length was estimated to be 1.9 miles.
Reports of trees and power lines down.
Reports of trees and power lines down.
Reports of trees and power lines down.
Storage building flipped over, single wide trailer moved off its blocks with awning removed and damage to green houses near the community of Clay off Hwy 818.

This brief and weak EF-1 tornado traveled through northwest Jackson Parish and did a mix of low end structural damage to mobile homes while uprooting hardwood and softwood trees. Peak width was 100 yards with a peak wind speed of 90 mph. The path length was 1.3 miles.
Public reported trees down south of Gilmer.
Downed tree limbs and car cover partially blown off at a residence in Gilmer. Location is approximate.
Roof damage at gas station at intersection of TX 300 and FM 1844.
A 72 mph wind gust was reported on the Hwy 6 bridge over Toledo Bend.
Large limb fell and shattered vehicle windshield near the intersection of Hwy 71 and Union Grove Cemetery Road.
There are a couple of trees down on Hwy 165 between Olla and Urania near the Lasalle Correctional Center, reported by 911 Dispatch.
